significant_events.summary: Promoting harmonious coexistence, educating on responsible cat ownership, controlling stray cat population via adoption/neutering, and advocating for animal welfare legislation.

significant_events.event_name: Stray Cat Rescue and Lifetime Care
significant_events.source_quote: Cat Society Hong Kong is a no kill organisation, the cats rescued are provided with food, shelter and medical care for the rest of their lives.
significant_events.summary: Rescuing stray cats from the streets, including disabled, ill, abused, or old ones, and providing them with food, shelter, and medical care for life.

significant_events.event_name: Legislative Council Engagement on Pet Trading
significant_events.source_quote: The Society suggested cat owners in PRH estates not to bring their cats to public areas, so to avoid problems such as losing cats and frightening of cats. The organisation also shared their views on pet trading in Legislative Council meeting.
significant_events.summary: The Society shared its views on pet trading in a Legislative Council meeting.

significant_events.source_quote: The organisation has hosted joint promotion campaigns aimed at raising awareness about stray cats and promoting responsible cat ownership among the general public.
significant_events.summary: Hosted campaigns to raise awareness about stray cats and promote responsible cat ownership among the general public.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2011, the Cat Society Hong Kong conducted "Cats on Facebook" campaign to promote and encourage adoption services, within a month, all 50 cats involved in this campaign were adopted.
significant_events.summary: Campaign to promote and encourage adoption services, resulting in 50 adoptions within a month.

data.original_text: The founding purpose of the organisation is to promote the protection of the rights of cats, foster the belief that humans and cats can coexist harmoniously in our shared communities, and educate the public on responsible cat ownership, aiming to control the population of stray cats through adoption and neutering programs, and prevent and/or assist with cases of cat abuse by advocating for animal welfare legislation.
They welcome volunteers to help care for the group of cats they house and provide medical care for in their To Kwa Wan shelter.
Cat Society Hong Kong is a no kill organisation, the cats rescued are provided with food, shelter and medical care for the rest of their lives.
Adoption, homing & rescue. Rescuing stray cats from the streets, some of which are physically disabled, chronically ill, have been abused and have character defects, or are too old to be adopted.
In 2011, the Cat Society Hong Kong conducted "Cats on Facebook" campaign to promote and encourage adoption services, within a month, all 50 cats involved in this campaign were adopted.
The organisation has hosted joint promotion campaigns aimed at raising awareness about stray cats and promoting responsible cat ownership among the general public.

Home visit volunteers: to pay home visit to the new owner's house before the cat comes home; Photo volunteers: take monthly photos of cats in the cattery or in foster homes; Designers: design posters, Leaflets and Timeline Banners for the Cat Club;
Cat house volunteers: to help with cleaning the cattery, basic care (such as bathing), feeding the cats, and administering medication.

The Society suggested cat owners in PRH estates not to bring their cats to public areas, so to avoid problems such as losing cats and frightening of cats. The organisation also shared their views on pet trading in Legislative Council meeting.
香港群貓會於2007年成立，是一個100%由義工負責營運的註冊慈善團體，旨在保障貓隻的權益，多方面推廣人與貓在社區和諧共處的信念，教育飼養貓隻的正確觀，並透過領養或絕育有效控制流浪貓隻的數量，以及避免任何不人道對待貓隻的情況。

aliases: 香港群貓會Cat Society Hong Kong
香港群貓會Cats Society (Hong Kong) Ltd
